project.name: Personalized Gym Agent
project.description: A personalized gym training agent: agent diagram coordinating user models for tailored workout planning.

63a2c160-ebbb-457b-953a-93fd2e643224.name: I am here to answer any questions regarding exercises, nutrition and recovery.

1f369122-06c2-4050-ba54-4ea160f95a8c.name: I focus on gym and fitness topics only dude.

8dba49ff-2fc1-4066-b124-19e2df7981e2.name: Muscles_intent
8dba49ff-2fc1-4066-b124-19e2df7981e2.intent_description: Question about how to become muscular and which exercises to perform.

aa261be5-9c53-4df1-a72f-5a107ce8f1b7.name: Focus on heavy compound lifts like squats, deadlifts, bench press, overhead press, and rows to build overall muscle mass.

747b8558-325d-4a15-baba-0d18a6602a8d.name: Train 3–5 times per week, progressively increasing the weight while eating enough protein and calories to support growth.

900f7fd9-738a-4981-a7d9-6c7334621981.name: Get good sleep and stay consistent—muscle comes from steady effort over time.

ad226172-93d8-4644-80c5-62825565dd24.name: Hi, I am your buddy, the fitness agent.

bb7e67bd-ead9-4f14-93db-c18af0d806af.name: Nutrition basics: Eat mostly whole foods (lean protein, vegetables, fruits, whole grains, healthy fats). Protein: ~1.6–2.2 g per kg of body weight daily..

13072ea8-9b51-45fe-927e-a9323627701b.name: Match calories to your goal: slight surplus to gain muscle, deficit to lose fat, maintenance to stay the same.

95241c3a-f8a2-4bf0-b169-5dcff5a926fb.name: Carbs fuel training; fats support hormones.

44904a03-ac46-4f9f-983a-f6a740b81c0e.name: Drink plenty of water and limit ultra-processed foods and alcohol.

90dfba4f-6dec-4ca8-8c15-786ed36587a1.name: Be consistent — results come from habits, not perfection

37dff3e2-013c-42f0-ad6e-bbc197247d14.name: Other
37dff3e2-013c-42f0-ad6e-bbc197247d14.intent_description: Any question that does not fit in "Muscles" or "Nutrition"

566a781d-eb9d-4f80-bd98-8882ed800717.name: Muscles_intent
566a781d-eb9d-4f80-bd98-8882ed800717.intent_description: Question about how to become muscular and which exercises to perform.

4037bf46-f138-4c9c-a891-c8f5639a19a0.name: Other
4037bf46-f138-4c9c-a891-c8f5639a19a0.intent_description: Any question that does not fit in "Muscles" or "Nutrition"

e082c986-9bb3-45be-8f43-2e6b8af7a9d7.name: Hi! I’m your fitness helper and guide.

ac420544-4ccf-4a49-9b3f-0c1eb9278f7e.name: I can answer questions about workouts, food, and recovery.

3e3e5cbe-d409-4601-9cb2-11b371cfb064.name: I only talk about gym and fitness.

bb125833-aa05-440d-922a-1684c5faea6f.name: Focus on big, heavy lifts to build muscle: squats, deadlifts, bench press, overhead press, and rows.

4403443a-176c-4c34-b4c1-59566a6cee15.name: Train 3–5 days a week. Add a little weight over time. Eat enough protein and calories to grow.

f7f7c51d-0768-4d06-ab4d-fe498e2665e7.name: Sleep well and be consistent. Muscle grows with steady effort over time.

741a9bfa-d1ca-4ecf-b131-c0648fc0652a.name: Eat mostly whole foods: lean protein, vegetables, fruit, whole grains, and healthy fats. Aim for 1.6 to 2.2 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight each day.

7c1a4721-5f81-4330-b2a7-b0e395c52aa3.name: Match calories to your goal: eat a little more to gain muscle, eat less to lose fat, eat the same to stay the same.

8c518247-6c3f-418a-9384-bb5272521b2b.name: Carbs give you energy for training. Fats help your hormones.

8c96e0a0-670e-4c28-9ef0-b81fef0b1e8d.name: Drink plenty of water. Limit highly processed foods and alcohol.

2c319d4a-9344-4a61-95d8-134fd903c053.name: Be consistent. Results come from good habits, not perfection.

c383f8b0-d915-439b-8bde-18a30da80fa3.name: Muscles_intent
c383f8b0-d915-439b-8bde-18a30da80fa3.intent_description: Question about how to become muscular and which exercises to perform.

90d28305-378e-4bad-b3ea-61c3ddaa5921.name: Other
90d28305-378e-4bad-b3ea-61c3ddaa5921.intent_description: Any question that does not fit in "Muscles" or "Nutrition"

82783f5d-8ef3-47f7-8feb-9651bdf73d63.name: Hi, I am your buddy, the fitness agent.

d91b1fe8-cd6c-4b20-928c-70021c0f044d.name: I am here to answer any questions regarding exercises, nutrition and recovery.

10a044fe-f0bd-41e8-ae27-0f2c2afc2cac.name: I focus on gym and fitness topics only dude.

d55e541d-6530-44ad-8d86-0ae51039bd72.name: Focus on accessible compound upper-body and core movements like bench press or machine chest press, overhead press, seated rows, lat pulldowns/assisted pull-ups, dips or triceps pushdowns, face pulls, and anti-rotation core work (e.g., Pallof press), using seated or strap-stabilized setups as needed.

964f437d-ab33-4e0d-bd1c-e36387c879eb.name: Train 3–5 times per week, progressively increasing resistance on upper-body and core-focused exercises while eating enough protein and calories to support growth; balance pushing and pulling to protect your shoulders and allow adequate recovery.

31249e62-6b58-48fb-af98-e55090d683e1.name: Get good sleep and stay consistent—muscle comes from steady effort over time.

3fa5c3b5-3e12-4968-b16c-1f80a98693c1.name: Nutrition basics: Eat mostly whole foods (lean protein, vegetables, fruits, whole grains, healthy fats). Protein: ~1.6–2.2 g per kg of body weight daily..

5c6dad70-8209-4de6-ae7a-5a9747fada8a.name: Match calories to your goal: slight surplus to gain muscle, deficit to lose fat, maintenance to stay the same.

ac38d464-9f06-48cf-b6c5-76e721298f92.name: Carbs fuel training; fats support hormones.

49fc07e1-484c-4459-b5b9-b223c5d7b129.name: Drink plenty of water and limit ultra-processed foods and alcohol.

0bcfa6cb-74c0-43c8-867b-2fc966c53671.name: Be consistent — results come from habits, not perfection

63a2c160-ebbb-457b-953a-93fd2e643224.name: I am here to answer any questions regarding exercises, nutrition and recovery.

1f369122-06c2-4050-ba54-4ea160f95a8c.name: I focus on gym and fitness topics only dude.

8dba49ff-2fc1-4066-b124-19e2df7981e2.name: Muscles_intent
8dba49ff-2fc1-4066-b124-19e2df7981e2.intent_description: Question about how to become muscular and which exercises to perform.

aa261be5-9c53-4df1-a72f-5a107ce8f1b7.name: Focus on heavy compound lifts like squats, deadlifts, bench press, overhead press, and rows to build overall muscle mass.

747b8558-325d-4a15-baba-0d18a6602a8d.name: Train 3–5 times per week, progressively increasing the weight while eating enough protein and calories to support growth.

900f7fd9-738a-4981-a7d9-6c7334621981.name: Get good sleep and stay consistent—muscle comes from steady effort over time.

ad226172-93d8-4644-80c5-62825565dd24.name: Hi, I am your buddy, the fitness agent.

bb7e67bd-ead9-4f14-93db-c18af0d806af.name: Nutrition basics: Eat mostly whole foods (lean protein, vegetables, fruits, whole grains, healthy fats). Protein: ~1.6–2.2 g per kg of body weight daily..

13072ea8-9b51-45fe-927e-a9323627701b.name: Match calories to your goal: slight surplus to gain muscle, deficit to lose fat, maintenance to stay the same.

95241c3a-f8a2-4bf0-b169-5dcff5a926fb.name: Carbs fuel training; fats support hormones.

44904a03-ac46-4f9f-983a-f6a740b81c0e.name: Drink plenty of water and limit ultra-processed foods and alcohol.

90dfba4f-6dec-4ca8-8c15-786ed36587a1.name: Be consistent — results come from habits, not perfection

37dff3e2-013c-42f0-ad6e-bbc197247d14.name: Other
37dff3e2-013c-42f0-ad6e-bbc197247d14.intent_description: Any question that does not fit in "Muscles" or "Nutrition"
